methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $11M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 12 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Westmag is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $11M to date. The valuation exercise draws on a comp universe of nine private robotics firms (including 1X Technologies, Foundation Robotics, Divergent, Exotec, and Locus Robotics) and one public comparable, though the peer set remains narrow at this stage and sector.

Only one valuation method proved applicable given data constraints. Stage-Progression analysis applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier—standard for Seed to next-stage advancement—to the $11M capital raised, yielding an implied valuation of $28M. A sensitivity band around this central estimate, anchored to typical variance in stage-transition multiples, suggests a range of $23M (low) to $32M (high). The Peer-Median approach could not be applied; while 12 robotics and manufacturing peers exist in the broader sector, the narrow filters required for validity (same stage, adjacent stage, similar valuation tier) returned insufficient observations to support reliable benchmarking.

The $28M median valuation is supported by the stage-progression method alone. Key uncertainties include the absence of same-stage peer pricing data and limited visibility into Westmag's unit economics, capital efficiency, and technical differentiation relative to the identified peer set. Any refinement would require either additional seed-stage robotics comparables or direct disclosure of revenue or growth metrics from existing portfolio companies.
